seconds_v2.desc: Top-down on an empty magenta-pink glass jar on a white surface; several white cotton wicks stand inside, held upright by two crossed wooden skewers taped into an X across the rim to keep them from leaning.
Camera shifts slightly over the same pink jar; the taped wooden-skewer X spans the opening and the white wicks droop and splay within the empty container.
Closer tilt on the empty pink jar; the crossed skewers and a small piece of tape are visible, with wicks anchored to the jar bottom in a loose triangle.
Top-down again on the empty magenta jar, white wicks visibly arranged toward a triangle pattern beneath the crossed skewer guides.
Steady overhead view of the empty pink glass jar, wooden-skewer X laid across the rim holding the bare white wicks centered and upright.
View tightens on the pink jar interior; the wicks hang straight down to the base, still no wax poured, skewers crossing above.
A black pouring pitcher edges into frame from the left toward the empty pink jar, the skewer X still bridging the rim over the dry wicks.
The black wax pitcher moves closer over the magenta jar's rim, poised to pour; wicks remain held in place by the taped crossed skewers.
Pitcher hovers at the edge of the empty pink jar just before pouring; white wicks still dry and upright under the wooden-skewer guides.
